constraints.expression: consumer cross-checks canonical_run_id against a known CanonicalRunSchema instance via consumer-side registry resolution; manifest emits CANONICAL_RUN_REF_CONTEXT_DEFERRED when the consumer's registry hasn't supplied context
constraints.message: canonical_run_id MUST resolve to a registered CanonicalRunSchema instance (FR-B8 reference resolution).
constraints.evaluation_note: Cross-record reference. The library validates the structural shape (non-empty string) at the structural layer; cross-record resolution is consumer-side because the registry context is not bundled into a single envelope. Runtime-deferred classification surfaces the obligation in the UnverifiedObligationsManifest. The CanonicalRunSchema lands in PR-A3.8 (FR-B1) — until then, consumers building EpicCheckpoint pipelines pre-allocate the registry context with the expected canonical_run_id values.

constraints.expression: consumer ensures (epic_id, canonical_run_id) is a stable composite key — the EpicCheckpoint chain MUST NOT reuse this composite across distinct EPIC instances
constraints.message: Composite key uniqueness invariant: the (epic_id, canonical_run_id) tuple is the EpicCheckpoint chain identity.
constraints.evaluation_note: Cross-record uniqueness. The schema validates each field's shape; the consumer-side persistence layer enforces the composite-key constraint. Runtime-deferred because cross-record state is required.
